27 April 2026 Military News

News Reports

Current Operations

    Other Operations

  • Coast Guard offloads over $53M in illicit drugs from the Eastern Pacific & Caribbean Sea US Coast Guard 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Coast Guard Cutter Escanaba's crew offloaded approximately 7,050 pounds of cocaine worth more than $53 million, Monday, at Port Everglades.

    • Operation Epic Fury / Operation Roaring Lion | Operation True Promise 4

  • 50 Iranian hospitals damaged in US-Israeli attacks: Health minister Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Health Minister Mohammad-Reza Zafarghandi says the United States and Israel carried out nearly 240 attacks on medical facilities in the Islamic Republic during the recent war, leaving at least 50 hospitals damaged.

  • White House Confirms Mulling New Iranian Peace Proposal RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt confirmed that US President Donald Trump discussed a new Iranian proposal to resolve the two-month-old war, now under a cease-fire, with his top security aides on April 27.
  • US Offers $10 Million For Information On Iran Proxy Commander RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- The US State Department, through its Rewards for Justice Program, is offering a reward of up to $10 million for information related to an Iraqi militia commander linked to Iran-backed militias operating in the country.
  • Trump Discusses With Security Team Iran's Proposal on Hormuz Strait Opening - White House Sputnik 27 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has discussed with his national security team Iran's proposal on the mutual reopening of the Strait of Hormuz, White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt said on Monday.
  • Trump Says Tehran 'Can Call Us' If It Wants To Talk, While Iran's Araqchi Arrives In Russia RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said that Tehran "can call us" if it wants to talk, but he added that it must give up any plans to build a nuclear weapon or "there's no reason to meet."
  • Trump says he canceled U.S. representatives' trip to Pakistan for talks with Iran People's Daily 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump announced on social media Saturday that he just canceled the trip of U.S. representatives going to Islamabad, Pakistan, to meet with the Iranian side.

  • With Peace Talks Stalled, Iranian Foreign Minister Meets Putin To Solidify Ties RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin voiced strong support for Tehran in its conflict with the United States and Israel as he greeted Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araqchi for talks in St. Petersburg on April 27.
  • Iran gives US new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran has given the US a new proposal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz and hold talks on Tehran's nuclear program at a later stage, Tass reported citing news website.
  • April 27: Iran strengthens its diplomatic hand as US faces mounting strategic setbacks Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran continues to consolidate gains on multiple strategic fronts, with the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) reaffirming the cohesion and battlefield resilience of the Axis of Resistance, while firmly dismissing Israeli claims of Hezbollah's military capabilities as unfounded.
  • Tehran slams US piracy on high seas as Washington illegally seizes Iranian oil tankers Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran has condemned the United States for the outright legalization of piracy and armed robbery on the high seas, following the interception of Iranian oil tankers by US forces under the pretext of so-called "seizure warrants."
  • Qalibaf Suggests Iran Could Escalate By Restricting Bab el-Mandeb Strait RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- Parliament speaker Mohammad Baqer Qalibaf, who led the first round of talks with the United States in Pakistan on April 11-12, warned in a post on X that Iran still holds strategic leverage despite the United States' "winning cards."
  • Araghchi Says Held Good Meeting With Putin, Discussed Conflict Between Israel, US, Iran Sputnik 27 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi said on Monday that he had good meeting with Russian President Vladimir Putin and discussed in detail the conflict between Israel, the United States and Iran.
  • Iranian FM says discussions in Oman include ways to ensure safe transit through Strait of Hormuz People's Daily 27 Apr 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Seyyed Abbas Araghchi said midnight Sunday that the discussions he had earlier in Muscat with Omani officials included ways to ensure safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz.
  • Araghchi arrives in Russia for close consultations on regional, international issues Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has arrived in Russia to continue close consultations between Tehran and Moscow on regional and international issues, and to enhance bilateral relations.

  • Safeguarding Waterways 'Test' of International Order, Secretary-General Tells Security Council, Urges Full Respect for International Law UN Security Council 27 Apr 2026 -- Following are UN Secretary-General António Guterres' remarks at the Security Council open debate on maritime security, in New York today:
  • Immediately Restore Freedom of Navigation through Strait of Hormuz, Speakers Urge in Security Council Condemning Critical Waterway's Use as 'Bargaining Chip' UN Security Council 27 Apr 2026 -- The Security Council today heard urgent calls to restore freedom of navigation through the Strait of Hormuz, with UN Secretary-General António Guterres warning that disruptions since early March have "struck" at global energy security, food supplies and trade. Speakers condemned the use of one of the world's most critical maritime chokepoints as a "bargaining chip" and an illegal assault on shipping, seafarers and the global economy.
  • Secretary-General's remarks to the Security Council - on Maritime Security UN Secretary-General 27 Apr 2026

  • China slams US 'abusive sanctions' on refineries over alleged links to Iran Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- China's Ministry of Foreign Affairs has denounced the US for imposing sanctions on Chinese refineries over alleged links to Iran.

  • Meeting with Foreign Minister of Iran Abbas Araghchi President of Russia 27 Apr 2026 -- President of Russia Vladimir Putin held a meeting with Special Representative of the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran, Minister of Foreign Affairs of the Islamic Republic of Iran Abbas Araghchi.
  • US must revise its nuclear stance to reach agreement with Iran: Russian envoy Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- A senior Russian diplomat says the United States needs to revise its policy on nuclear issues in order to open the door to an agreement with Iran.
  • Putin Meets Iran's Top Diplomat in St. Petersburg Sputnik 27 Apr 2026 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin held talks with Abbas Araghchi and said Moscow would do everything in its power, alongside regional states, to help bring peace to the Middle East as soon as possible.

  • Germany's Merz: US 'humiliated' by Iranian leadership Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz says the United States is being "humiliated" by the Iranian leadership.
  • Germany's Merz Says US Lacks 'Strategy' For Iran War RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- German Chancellor Friedrich Merz said on April 27 that the United States lacks a clear strategy in the war with Iran and warned that the situation may not end anytime soon.
  • We must step up efforts together to safeguard international waterways from disruption: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 27 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Steven Doughty MP, Minister of State for Europe, North America, and the Overseas Territories, at the UN Security Council Meeting on the safety and protection of waterways in the maritime domain.

  • Analyst: Putin May Try To Push Iran Into Nuclear Concessions RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- Exiled Iranian political analyst Amir Chahaki told RFE/RL's Radio Farda that Russian President Vladimir Putin may seek to push Iran to make concessions in negotiations with the United States as a way of currying favor with US President Donald Trump.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • US, Royal Moroccan Armed Forces launch African Lion 26 in Morocco Army News 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Africa Command's largest annual joint exercise, African Lion 26, has officially begun in Morocco. The exercise is hosted across four nations including Ghana, Morocco, Senegal and Tunisia. The activities in Morocco will include approximately 5,000 personnel from over 40 countries and more than 30 U.S.-based industry partners validating future warfare capabilities across multiple locations within the country from April 27 to May 8, 2026.
  • Sword: The Army's signature exercise in Europe for Warfighting with Allies Army News 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Army Europe and Africa kicked off exercise Sword 26 on April 27, 2026. This series of exercises will run through the end of May 2026 in eight countries across NATO's Eastern Flank, replacing the DEFENDER exercise series.
  • Blue Ridge Returns to Yokosuka After Spring Patrol U.S. 7th Fleet 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. 7th Fleet flagship USS Blue Ridge (LCC 19) returned to Commander, Fleet Activities Yokosuka, Japan, April 23, marking the completion of the spring patrol in the Indo-Pacific region.
  • Submarine Squadron 15 Supports Inalåhan Recovery Following Super Typhoon Sinlaku U.S. 7th Fleet 27 Apr 2026 -- In the wake of Super Typhoon Sinlaku, Sailors assigned to Commander, Submarine Squadron 15 (CSS-15), its forward-deployed submarines, and Performance Monitoring Team Detachment Guam partnered with the village of Inalåhan to support recovery efforts, April 23, 2026.
  • US, Moroccan Air Forces Project Combat Power During Exercise African Lion 26 US Air Force 27 Apr 2026 -- A combined flight of two U.S. Air Force B-52H Stratofortress bombers and four Royal Moroccan Air Force F-16 Fighting Falcons, signaled the launch of the country's first accredited joint terminal attack controller qualification course, April 23.
  • 95th FS, FGS enhance lethality through advanced threat training during Ready Tiger 26-3 US Air Force 27 Apr 2026 -- Airmen and F-35A Lightning II aircraft from the 95th Fighter and Fighter Generation Squadrons deployed, April 13-17, to Savannah Air National Guard Base, Georgia, to enhance combat capability and lethality during exercise Ready Tiger 26-3.
  • FRCE delivers first metal 3D printed parts to fleet US Navy 27 Apr 2026 -- Fleet Readiness Center East (FRCE) is marking a new era of aircraft sustainment with the delivery of its first flight-certified metal additive manufactured parts to the fleet, significantly improving aircraft downtime and flight line readiness.
  • Balikatan 2026: DESRON-7, Task Force Ashland Commence Multinational Maritime Event US Navy 27 Apr 2026 -- Members of Destroyer Squadron (DESRON) 7 and Task Force (TF) Ashland, composed of Whidbey Island-class amphibious dock landing ship USS Ashland (LSD 48) and embarked Marines from I Marine Expeditionary Force, began participation in Exercise Balikatan 2026, April 20.
  • Navy Commissions Submarine USS Idaho US Navy 27 Apr 2026 -- The Navy commissioned the Virginia-class fast-attack submarine USS Idaho (SSN 799) in a traditional ceremony held Saturday, April 25, at Naval Submarine Base New London in Groton, Connecticut.
  • MQ-25A Stingray achieves successful first flight, advancing future of naval aviation US Navy 27 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Navy's MQ-25A Stingray™ successfully completed its first test flight from Boeing's facility at MidAmerica Airport in Mascoutah, Illinois, April 25, achieving a key step for unmanned carrier operations.
  • Securing the ultimate high ground: how the Space Force is revolutionizing airborne target tracking United States Space Force 27 Apr 2026 -- In the modern era of emerging and pacing threats, the ability to clearly see the battlespace is no longer a given. As adversaries develop increasingly sophisticated ways to deny and contest traditional airspace, the U.S. military is looking upward to maintain its tactical advantage.
  • Eyes on the Horizon: Flintlock Integrates Drone Course to Meet Evolving Threats AFRICOM 27 Apr 2026 -- During Exercise Flintlock 26 in Jacqueville, international and African forces took part in a new drone training course aimed at preparing for modern security threats.
  • Balikatan 2026: U.S. Marines with MRF-D 26 conduct counter-landing live-fire exercise USMC News 27 Apr 2026 -- U.S. Marines with Marine Rotational Force - Darwin participated in a combined counter-landing live-fire exercise alongside service members from the Armed Forces of the Philippines, Australia Defence Force and New Zealand Defence Force, April 27, 2026.

  • NATO Deputy Secretary General visits United Kingdom to discuss defence innovation NATO 27 Apr 2026 -- On Monday (27 April 2026), NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska visited the United Kingdom to participate in the inauguration of NATO's Defence Innovation Accelerator for the North Atlantic (DIANA) Capital Network event in London, and to engage with students at Oxford University.

  • Andrei Belousov arrives in Kyrgyzstan to attend meeting of SCO Defence Ministers Russia MoD 27 Apr 2026 -- Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov has arrived in Bishkek, where he is going to take part in a meeting of defence ministers of the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO), as well as inspect Russian military facilities located on the territory of Kyrgyzstan.
  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov holds talks with Iranian Deputy Defence Minister Russia MoD 27 Apr 2026 -- Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov held talks with Brigadier General Reza Talaei-Nik, the Deputy Minister of Defence for Strategic Planning of the Islamic Republic of Iran.
  • Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov inspects Russian Defence Ministry facilities in Kyrgyzstan Russia MoD 27 Apr 2026 -- Russian Defence Minister Andrei Belousov, as part of a working visit to Kyrgyzstan, inspected facilities of the Russian military base deployed in the Republic.

  • We Are Actively Seeking Ways to Secure Ballistic Missile Defense Capabilities and Other Air Defense Systems Against Missiles - Address by the President President of Ukraine 27 Apr 2026
  • For the first time, Ukraine Recovery Conference will include a dedicated security and defense dimension: Yulia Svyrydenko Ukraine Government 27 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine and Poland are preparing to host the Ukraine Recovery Conference this June. For the first time, Ukraine Recovery Conference will include a dedicated security and defense dimension.
  • Undersecretary Martin Roger emphasised the importance of NATO unity and continued support for Ukraine in meetings in Washington Estonia MFA 27 Apr 2026 -- From 21 to 24 April, Martin Roger, Undersecretary for Political Affairs at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, visited Washington DC, where he met with his counterpart, Allison Hooker, Under Secretary of State for Political Affairs at the U.S. Department of State, as well as other representatives of the U.S. government, both at the State Department and the National Security Council. Meetings were also held at Congress and in think tanks.
  • Signing of agreement to build Ukrainian drones in Norway Government of Norway 27 Apr 2026 -- Norway continues its support for Ukraine. The partnership between Ukraine and Norway is becoming even closer. Now, a set of agreements are signed, marking the start of production of Ukrainian drones in Norway.
  • Ukraine and Norway launch joint production of mid-strike drones Ukraine MOD 27 Apr 2026 -- Ukraine and Norway are establishing their first joint production of Ukrainian UAVs. Several thousand mid-strike drones are planned to be manufactured in Norway. All products manufactured under the project will be delivered to the Defence Forces of Ukraine.
  • Starving Soldiers: Ukrainian Army's New Manpower Struggle RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- For months, Ukrainian soldiers battled to capture and then hold the Kharkiv region city of Kupyansk, split by the Oskil River and home to a crucial, now-unused rail line stretching to the west.

  • Chinese and Kyrgyz defense ministers discuss bilateral military cooperation PRC MND 27 Apr 2026 -- Chinese Minister of National Defense, Dong Jun, held talks with Kyrgyz Minister of Defense, Mukambetov Ruslan Mustafaevich, on Monday during his invited visit to Kyrgyzstan.

  • AIT chief urges Taiwan to pass 'comprehensive' defense budget CNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The top U.S. representative to Taiwan on Monday urged lawmakers to approve a "comprehensive" defense budget, as the Legislature prepared to resume cross-party negotiations on a Cabinet-proposed NT$1.25 trillion (US$39.77 billion) special spending bill.
  • Final batch of 28 M1A2T Abrams tanks delivered to Taiwan CNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The final batch of M1A2T Abrams tanks Taiwan purchased from the United States arrived at Taipei Port late Sunday and were transported early Monday to the Army's Armored Training Command in Hukou Township, Hsinchu County.

  • Securing advanced manufacturing in Bendigo with new Bushmasters Australia DOD 27 Apr 2026 -- The Albanese Government is investing $750 million to deliver a pipeline of Bushmasters for the Australian Defence Force's (ADF) fleet of protected mobility vehicles in regional Victoria, supporting around 300 local jobs for the next seven years.

  • Raksha Mantri to lead Indian delegation at SCO Defence Ministers' Meeting in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan India PIB 27 Apr 2026 -- Raksha Mantri Shri Rajnath Singh will lead a high-level Indian delegation to the Shanghai Cooperation Organisation (SCO) Defence Ministers' Meeting which will be held in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan on April 28, 2026. During the meeting, the Defence Ministers of various member countries will deliberate upon a number of issues concerning the defence & security of the region. Issues related to international peace, counter-terrorism and defence cooperation among SCO member states will also be discussed.

  • Turkish Land Forces Commander pays official visit to Azerbaijan Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- On April 27, First Deputy Minister of Defense, Chief of the General Staff of the Azerbaijan Army, Colonel General Karim Valiyev met with a delegation led by Commander of the Land Forces of the Republic of Türkiye, Army General Metin Tokel, who is on an official visit to the country, Azerbaijan's Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.

  • As warheads proliferate, decades old nuclear weapons treaty must evolve, warns UN chief UN News 27 Apr 2026 -- With top diplomats gathered at UN Headquarters to review the 1970 Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT), the UN Secretary-General warned that it must evolve to survive the age of AI and other new technologies.
  • Secretary-General's remarks to the Eleventh Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT) UN Secretary-General 27 Apr 2026
  • This Treaty has been a truly vital pillar of international peace and security: UK Statement at the UN General Assembly UK FCDO 27 Apr 2026 -- Statement by Steven Doughty MP, Minister of State for Europe, North America, and the Overseas Territories, at the UN General Assembly meeting on the Review Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons (NPT).

  • Tunisia - Border Security Project Phase III US Dept. of State 27 Apr 2026 -- The U.S. Department of State has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Tunisia to buy equipment in support of its Border Security Project Phase III. The total estimated cost is $95 million.

  • President: Negotiations are underway on specific projects regarding the joint production of military-grade products Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- "We purchase many military-grade products from the Czech Republic, and at the current stage, negotiations are underway on specific projects regarding joint production," said President Ilham Aliyev during a press statement with Czech Prime Minister Andrej Babiš.
  • "TurAz Qartalı-2026" joint tactical flight exercise kicks off in Azerbaijan Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- Within the military cooperation between the Republic of Azerbaijan and the Republic of Türkiye, the "TurAz Qartalı-2026" joint tactical flight exercise commenced in Azerbaijan, the Ministry of Defense told AZERTAC.
  • Azerbaijan, Ethiopia to cooperate in defense sector Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- Azerbaijan is set to cooperate with Ethiopia in the defense sector.

  • OCCAR-EA Director signs In-Service Support contract-extension for MU-90 Light-Weight Torpedo (LWT) OCCAR 27 Apr 2026 -- 27 April 2026 OCCAR-EA has awarded a Contract Amendment on behalf of the Light Weight Torpedo LWT Programme Participating States Australia, France, Italy and Germany.

  • Aurora 26 is underway Swedish Armed Forces 27 Apr 2026 -- Last week, allied forces from several countries arrived. The Swedish-led exercise Aurora 26 has now begun.

  • Asian countries see largest annual rise in military spending since 2009 Azerbaijan State News Agency - (AZERTAC) 27 Apr 2026 -- Military expenditure in Asia and Oceana totaled $681 billion in 2025, up 8.1% from the previous year and the largest annual rise since 2009, Anadolu Agency reported citing the Stockholm International Peace Research Institute (SIPRI).

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio With Trey Yingst of Fox News Channel US Dept. of State 27 Apr 2026

  • 27 April 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 27 Apr 2026

  • FM Sa'ar meets with UN Special Coordinator for Lebanon Jeanine Hennis in Jerusalem Israel MFA 27 Apr 2026 -- "The Government of Lebanon must act decisively against Hezbollah and its terrorists, including through financial measures targeting its sources of funding."

  • PM Netanyahu at IDF Senior Command Conference Israel - Prime Minister 27 Apr 2026
  • Israeli soldier killed as helicopter targeted in Hezbollah drone strike Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- An Israeli soldier has been killed as a military helicopter sent to airlift wounded troops was targeted after fighters from the Lebanese Hezbollah resistance movement launched a drone strike against the occupation forces.

  • Israel destroys power, water infrastructure of Christian-majority town in south Lebanon Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Israeli forces have destroyed the power and water systems of a Christian town in southern Lebanon, as the regime continues to violate a ceasefire between the two sides.

  • Sheikh Qassem: Lebanon truce impossible without Iran's push in Islamabad talks Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Hezbollah's secretary general says Iran's insistence on ending Israeli aggression against Lebanon during indirect negotiations with Washington was the decisive factor that made a ceasefire with Israel possible.

  • All resistance fighters standing by Hezbollah stronger than ever: IRGC Quds Force chief Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- The chief commander of the Quds Force of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) has categorically dismissed Israeli propaganda aimed at downplaying Hezbollah's power, while underlining regional resistance forces' all-out support for the movement.

    • Other Conflicts

  • PH Navy holds maritime patrol to secure Mindanao waters Philippine News Agency 27 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) on Monday said one of its patrol ships, the BRP Artemio Ricarte (PS-37), conducted maritime patrol (MARPAT) operations as part of its efforts to secure the southeastern waters of Mindanao last April 26.

  • Restrictions on girls' education and women's employment in Afghanistan could lead to a loss of over 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030 - UNICEF UNICEF 27 Apr 2026 -- Afghanistan risks losing up to 20,000 women teachers and 5,400 healthcare workers by 2030 as restrictions on girls' education and women's employment continue, according to a new UNICEF analysis.
  • Afghanistan risks losing 25,000 women teachers and health workers UN News 27 Apr 2026 -- Restrictions on girls' education and women's employment in Afghanistan could leave the country with a deficit of over 25,000 female teachers and health workers by 2030, the UN Children's Fund (UNICEF) warned on Tuesday.

  • Malian Defense Minister Killed in Terrorist Attack on His Residence Sputnik 27 Apr 2026 -- Malian Defense Minister Sadio Camara was killed as a result of a terrorist attack on his residence, Malian government spokesman Issa Coulibaly said on Sunday.
  • Central Sahel: Millions of children in humanitarian need, UNICEF official warns UN News 27 Apr 2026 -- Nearly 7.5 million children across the Central Sahel region in Africa are in urgent need of humanitarian assistance - "an emergency that remains too far from the attention of the international community," a senior official with the UN child rights agency UNICEF has said.
  • Iran condemns terrorist attacks in Mali, assassination of defense minister Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Iran has strongly condemned recent terrorist attacks in Mali, which led to the assassination of Defense Minister Sadio Camara, members of his family, and a large number of civilians, including worshippers at a mosque in the city of Kati near the capital Bamako.
  • Spanish Government condemns jihadist attacks in Mali Spain MFA 27 Apr 2026

  • PH Army ready to face CHR probe over Negros Occidental clash Philippine News Agency 27 Apr 2026 -- The Philippine Army (PA) said it is more than willing to face an investigation by the Commission on Human Rights (CHR) into the April 19 clash against the New People's Army (NPA) in Toboso, Negros Occidental.

  • Spanish Government condemns violence in Colombia Spain MFA 27 Apr 2026

News Reports

  • Suspect in White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting Charged with Attempt to Assassinate the President US Dept. of Justice 27 Apr 2026 -- Cole Tomas Allen, 31, of Torrance, California, was arraigned today in U.S. District Court on charges stemming from the April 25, 2026, shooting at the White House Correspondents' Association Dinner, announced the Department of Justice.
  • Suspect In Washington Shooting Charged With Assassination Attempt On Trump RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- The suspect in the shooting at a Washington gala where US President Donald Trump was due to speak has been charged with attempted assassination as well as with a broader plot targeting senior government officials.
  • DC Shooting Suspect Due To Face First Court Appearance On April 27 RFE/RL 27 Apr 2026 -- The suspect in the shooting at a Washington gala where President Donald Trump was due to speak on April 25 is scheduled to make a court appearance on April 27, US officials said, as law-enforcement authorities seek to determine a motive for the attack by the heavily armed man.
  • DC gala shooting suspect voices grievances against US administration in writings to family: media reports Global Times 27 Apr 2026 -- The man accused of opening fire at the White House Correspondents' Association dinner railed against policies of the US administration and referred to himself as a "Friendly Federal Assassin" in writings sent to family members minutes before an attack that authorities increasingly believe was politically motivated, according to a message reviewed by The Associated Press (AP).

  • Prolific Chinese State-Sponsored Contract Hacker Extradited from Italy US Dept. of Justice 27 Apr 2026 -- Xu Zewei (徐泽伟), 34, of the People's Republic of China was extradited to the United States this weekend and appeared today in U.S. District Court in Houston on a nine-count indictment related to his involvement in computer intrusions between February 2020 and June 2021.

  • Practicing today for tomorrow's emergencies - WHO convenes countries and partners to simulate response to major disease outbreak WHO 27 Apr 2026 -- The World Health Organization (WHO) wrapped up Exercise Polaris II, a 2-day high-level simulation exercise, based around an outbreak of a fictional new bacterium spreading across the world.

  • Meeting with members of the Council of Legislators President of Russia 27 Apr 2026 -- On the Day of Russian Parliamentarism, the President addressed the Council of Legislators of the Russian Federation Federal Assembly.
  • Meeting with Federation Council Speaker Valentina Matviyenko President of Russia 27 Apr 2026 -- Following his address at the Council of Legislators meeting, Vladimir Putin held a working meeting with Speaker of the Federation Council Valentina Matviyenko.
  • Foreign Ministry statement on Russia's response to EU's 20th package of sanctions Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 27 Apr 2026
  • Press release on the summoning of the German Ambassador to the Foreign Ministry Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 27 Apr 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on April 27, 2026 PRC MofA 27 Apr 2026
  • EU's Industrial Acceleration Act creates investment barriers, discrimination; China to defend firms' rights: MOFCOM Global Times 27 Apr 2026 -- The EU's Industrial Acceleration Act imposes numerous restrictive requirements on foreign investment in four key emerging strategic sectors — batteries, electric vehicles, photovoltaics and critical raw materials — and introduces "EU origin" exclusivity clauses in public procurement and public support policies, constituting serious investment barriers and institutional discrimination, a spokesperson for China's Ministry of Commerce (MOFCOM) said on Monday.

  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 27 Apr 2026

  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un's Speech at Inauguration Ceremony of Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at Overseas Military Operations KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un made a speech at the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • DPRK Ministry of National Defence Gives Dinner Parties for Bereaved Families of KPA Martyrs in Special Military Operations KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defence of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea gave dinner parties for the bereaved families of martyrs on Sunday evening on the occasion of the inauguration of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
    • Democratic People's Republic of Korea gave dinner parties for the bereaved families of martyrs on Sunday evening on the occasion of the inauguration of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Russia's Official Delegation Visits Victorious Fatherland Liberation War Museum KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- An official delegation of the Russian Federation led by Vyacheslav Volodin, speaker of the State Duma, visited the Victorious Fatherland Liberation War Museum on April 26.
  • Russian Defense Minister Arrives KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- At the invitation of the Supreme People's Assembly and the Ministry of National Defence of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Andrei Beloussov, minister of Defense of the Russian Federation, and his party arrived here on April 26 to attend the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Russian Minister of Defense Leaves Here KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- Andrei Beloussov, minister of Defense of the Russian Federation, and his party left here on Monday after taking part in the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Russian State Commendation to Servicepersons of KPA Overseas Operations Units KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- Under a presidential decree of the Russian Federation, Order of Bravery was conferred on the commanding officers and combatants of the overseas operations units of the Korean People's Army who made distinguished contributions to liberating the territory of the Russian Federation from the military occupation by the Ukrainian neo-Nazi forces.
  • Inauguration Ceremony of Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at Overseas Military Operations Solemnly Held KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The magnificent memorial museum of combat feats was erected in the best place of the capital city to praise the great heroic spirit of the brave Korean People's Army soldiers involved in overseas military operations who created the legend of miraculous victory unprecedented in history with ardent patriotism and loyalty to defend the sacred prestige and dignity of the DPRK.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Meets Speaker of State Duma of Russian Federation KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, on Sunday met Vyacheslav Volodin, speaker of the State Duma of the Russian Federation, on a visit to the DPRK to attend the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un Meets Russian Defense Minister KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un , General Secretary of the Workers' Party of Korea and President of the State Affairs of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, on Sunday met Andrei Beloussov, minister of Defense of the Russian Federation, on a visit to the DPRK to attend the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Memorial Concert Given for War Martyrs at Overseas Military Operations KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- A memorial concert "Stars of the Motherland" for the war martyrs, excellent sons of the glorious Democratic People's Republic of Korea, was held outside the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations, built as a monumental edifice in the great era, on Sunday evening.
  • Dinner Given for Official Delegation of Russian Federation KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The State Affairs Commission of the Democratic People's Republic of Korea hosted a dinner on April 26 in welcome of Vyacheslav Volodin, speaker of the State Duma, Andrei Beloussov, minister of Defense, and other members of an official delegation of the Russian Federation who participated in the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations.
  • Letter to Respected Comrade Kim Jong Un and Participants in Inauguration Ceremony of Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at Overseas Military Operations from Russian President KCNA 27 Apr 2026 -- The respected Comrade Kim Jong Un and the participants in the inauguration ceremony of the Memorial Museum of Combat Feats at the Overseas Military Operations received a letter from Comrade Vladimir Vladimirovich Putin, president of the Russian Federation.

  • After Russia, India to be the second country in the world operating a commercial-level Fast Breeder Reactor; an indigenously designed Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) achieving first criticality is a significant milestone: Dr Jitendra Singh India PIB 27 Apr 2026 -- Union Minister Dr Jitendra Singh today said that after Russia, India will be the second country in the world operating a commercial-level fast breeder reactor. Addressing a workshop of MPs and MLAs, on "Small Modular Reactors" the Minister elaborated that India has achieved a significant milestone by developing an indigenously designed 500 MWe Prototype Fast Breeder Reactor (PFBR) at Kalpakkam, Tamil Nadu, which achieved first criticality on April 6, 2026.

  • France and Sweden held a meeting in the Nuclear Steering Group in Paris. Élysée - President of France 27 Apr 2026
  • The Daily Press Briefing (04/27/2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 27 Apr 2026

  • Statement by Minister of State Hahn on his arrival in Brunei for the ASEAN-EU Ministerial Meeting (27 and 28 April 2026) Germany Federal Foreign Office 27 Apr 2026
  • Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for the UN in New York Germany Federal Foreign Office 27 Apr 2026

  • President Herzog welcomed by President Kassym-Jomart Tokayev of Kazakhstan in Astana Israel MFA 27 Apr 2026 -- The leaders discussed deepening relations and advancing strategic cooperation between the two countries.
  • Former Israeli PMs join forces to dethrone Netanyahu in upcoming elections Press TV 27 Apr 2026 -- Two former Israeli prime ministers have said they would join forces in upcoming elections expected later this year in a bid to dethrone longtime prime minister Benjamin Netanyahu.

  • Prime Minister Carney announces the Canada Strong Fund - Canada's first sovereign wealth fund Prime Minister of Canada 27 Apr 2026 -- The world is increasingly dangerous, divided, and uncertain. In response, Canada's new government is focused on what we can control: building a stronger, more resilient, more independent Canadian economy.

  • Rapporteurs of European institutions address an open letter on the situation of local democracy in Türkiye Council of Europe 27 Apr 2026 -- Congress and Parliamentary Assembly among those to raise detention of Istanbul Mayor and other local elected representatives

  • Solomon Islands' political crisis will 'not fundamentally change' ties with Beijing Radio Free Asia 27 Apr 2026 -- Regardless of how the current political crisis in the Solomon Islands unfolds, experts told Radio Free Asia that they anticipate no change in the country's close-knit relationship with China.

  • World News in Brief: Sudan drone attacks condemned, South Sudan violence, airstrikes in Ukraine, South Africa Freedom Day UN News 27 Apr 2026 -- The United Nations has condemned two recent drone attacks in Sudan, one of which left seven dead, Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Monday during his regular media briefing in New York.
